title: Project membership change with conflict scan
difficulty_justification: Branching reasoning across rbac (existing memberships), slurm (running jobs the user owns), and docs (policy on conflicting projects); the recommendation depends on whether any conflict exists and what mitigation the policy mandates.
query_text: User jdoe was just added to project proj_y. What RBAC changes do I need to apply, and are there any conflicts with their existing project memberships I should resolve first?

eval_criteria.gold_answer: jdoe currently belongs to proj_x (highmem allocation, tier2_privileged) and adding proj_y (gpu allocation, tier1_public) creates one conflict: proj_y's default_account=proj_y_general would override proj_x as the SLURM default, breaking jdoe's two running highmem jobs (920501, 920514). Required RBAC changes: (a) add jdoe to proj_y_users group; (b) explicitly set jdoe's SLURM default_account back to proj_x; (c) grant proj_y allowed_partitions=[gpu, debug] without removing existing entitlements. Policy requires the affected user be notified of the change and given a 24h objection window before the membership update lands.

checkpoints.description: Agent retrieves jdoe's current project memberships.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: trace contains rbac.list_permissions or equivalent membership lookup

checkpoints.description: Agent checks jdoe's running jobs for impact.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: trace contains slurm.query_jobs filtered to user=jdoe

checkpoints.description: Recommendation calls out the default_account conflict and the explicit override fix.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer references default_account override and the affected jobs (920501, 920514)
